title: Who is Ansem? Who is FaZe Banks? What is Market Bubble?
text:
# Who is Ansem?

Ansem (@blknoiz06) is a crypto trader and one of the best-known KOLs (key opinion leaders) in the Solana ecosystem. He is a co-founder of Bullpen and co-hosts the "Market Bubble" podcast. The $ANSEM memecoin ("The Black Bull") is named after him.

# Who is FaZe Banks?

FaZe Banks is a content creator and a co-founder of FaZe Clan, one of the biggest names in gaming and creator culture. He co-hosts "Market Bubble" with Ansem. The podcast drives much of Bullpen's retail audience — viewers coming from Twitch, Kick, and YouTube.

# What is Market Bubble?

"Market Bubble" is the podcast hosted by Ansem and FaZe Banks covering crypto markets, trading culture, and the Solana ecosystem. Many new Bullpen users first hear about the terminal there.

title: What is $ANSEM (The Black Bull)?
text:
# What is $ANSEM?

$ANSEM, nicknamed "The Black Bull", is a memecoin on the Solana blockchain, named after the trader Ansem.

## Creator-fee airdrops

Trading $ANSEM generates creator fees. Those fees are airdropped back to the community through a claim page on Bullpen. Eligibility is based on social engagement — the wallet you connect is matched with your X (Twitter) activity.

## Claiming

1. Open the $ANSEM Claim page on Bullpen.
2. Connect your Solana wallet.
3. If eligible, your claimable amount appears automatically; confirm the claim transaction.

Claiming only ever requires a wallet connection and a transaction approval. Anyone who asks for your seed phrase to "process a claim" is running a scam.

$ANSEM is a memecoin: its value is driven by community and attention, it is highly volatile, and it can go to zero. This is informational only, not advice to buy or sell.

title: Crypto basics for new users
text:
# Crypto basics

## What is a wallet?

A crypto wallet is an app that holds the keys to your funds on a blockchain. On Solana, popular wallets include Phantom, Solflare, and Backpack. Your wallet has a public address (safe to share, like an account number) and a seed phrase (never share — it IS your money).

## What is a seed phrase?

A seed phrase (or recovery phrase) is a list of 12–24 words that can restore your entire wallet. Anyone who has it controls your funds. Never type it into a website, DM, or chat — Bullpen staff will never ask for it.

## What is bridging?

Bridging moves assets from one blockchain to another — for example moving USDC from Ethereum to Solana. You use a bridge app, pay a small fee, and receive the asset on the destination chain.

## What is gas?

Gas is the network fee paid to process a transaction. Solana fees are typically fractions of a cent.

## What is slippage?

Slippage is the difference between the price you expect and the price you get when a trade executes, common in fast-moving or low-liquidity markets.

## What is a memecoin?

A memecoin is a token whose value is driven mainly by community, culture, and attention rather than underlying cash flows. They are extremely volatile and can go to zero.

## What is liquidation?

When trading perps with leverage, liquidation happens if the price moves against your position far enough that your margin can no longer cover losses — the position is closed and the margin is lost.

title: $ANSEM claim troubleshooting — why does my claim show 0?
text:
# $ANSEM claim troubleshooting

## My claim shows 0 / "not eligible"

Eligibility is based on X (Twitter) engagement matched to the wallet you connect. A zero balance usually means one of:

- The connected wallet isn't the one linked to your eligible activity — try the wallet you intended to use.
- Your account didn't meet the engagement criteria for this round.
- Eligibility is checked per round; a new round may have different rules or timing.

For the exact, current eligibility rules and dates, check the official Bullpen claim page and announcements — those change over time and are the only authoritative source.

## I connected the wrong wallet

Disconnect the site in your wallet's **Connected apps** settings, switch to the correct wallet/account, and reconnect.

## The amount looks wrong or won't confirm

- Make sure you have a little **SOL** for the network fee.
- Refresh and retry; if a transaction is stuck, wait for it to clear before resubmitting.

## Important

Support can help you navigate the flow, but no one legitimate will ask for your seed phrase or ask you to send funds to "verify" or "unlock" a claim.

title: Staying safe — scams to watch for
text:
# Staying safe from scams

Crypto attracts scammers. Bullpen is non-custodial, so protecting your wallet is on you. These rules keep you safe.

## The one rule that matters most

**Never share your seed phrase / recovery phrase with anyone, for any reason.** Not with "support", not to "verify" a claim, not to "sync" a wallet. Bullpen and every legitimate app will NEVER ask for it. Anyone who does is stealing your funds.

## Common scams

- **Fake claim / airdrop sites** — a site or link (often in DMs, replies, or ads) that looks like the $ANSEM claim page but drains your wallet when you connect and approve. Always navigate to the official Bullpen domain yourself; never trust links from others.
- **Seed-phrase phishing** — a page or person asking you to "import", "validate", or "restore" your wallet by typing your 12–24 words. Never do this.
- **Fake support DMs** — someone messages you first claiming to be Bullpen/Ansem support. Real support does not DM you first asking for wallet access or seed phrases.
- **"Send X to receive Y"** — any claim that you must send funds first to receive an airdrop or unlock a bigger amount. Always a scam.
- **Malicious transaction approvals** — read what your wallet asks you to sign. A drainer tricks you into approving a transfer of your tokens. If a "claim" wants to move your assets out, reject it.

## If you think you're compromised

If you shared your seed phrase or approved a malicious transaction, assume the wallet is compromised: move any remaining funds to a fresh wallet (created on a clean device) immediately, and stop using the old one. A seed phrase, once exposed, can never be made safe again.
